finding: taskflow close --write can complete live-ledger close, target governance commit, and planning closeback commit, then still return failure with ATM_PLANNING_SOURCE_IDENTITY_DRIFT because the planning source content changed during its own closeback flow.
expectedBehavior: taskflow close --write should be atomic or idempotent: after successful side effects, it should return success, or a recovery/status result that recognizes the already-completed close instead of a blocking drift error.
evidenceOrRepro: ATM-GOV-0185 close on 2026-07-19: command `node atm.mjs taskflow close --task ATM-GOV-0185 --actor codex-governance-optimizer --historical-delivery a1d7fb9759aa1d2b684af71e907ef0df23195e3f --write --json` returned ATM_PLANNING_SOURCE_IDENTITY_DRIFT, while `node atm.mjs tasks status --task ATM-GOV-0185 --json` reported liveLedger done/released with no divergence; target commit 18da75e51 and planning commit fc36ae13 exist.
followUp: Closed by ATM-GOV-0236: `packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/close-side-effect-reconcile.ts` defines the idempotent close side-effect receipt contract. Pre-side-effect planning source identity drift remains fail-closed; post-side-effect drift is reported as reconciled with replayAllowed=false. ATM-GOV-0233 adds the paired transactional ticket completion receipt and duplicate side-effect queue-only guard; `node --strip-types tests/cli/taskflow-close-post-side-effect-idempotency.test.ts` and `node --strip-types tests/cli/taskflow-close-exactly-once-result.test.ts` are the passing receipts.
